summary: The user is preparing for a semester abroad at the University of California, Berkeley, having been accepted into the exchange program after applying on February 10th and receiving acceptance on March 20th. They are seeking advice on various aspects of their upcoming experience, including flight preparations, health insurance requirements, English language support services, and opportunities for involvement in student organizations and community service.
facts: The user is preparing for a semester abroad at the University of California, Berkeley.
The user submitted their application to the exchange program on February 10th and got accepted on March 20th.
The user has booked a flight to San Francisco scheduled to depart on April 1st and return on August 15th.
The user had an interview at the US embassy for their visa application on April 25th.
The user has been taking an intensive English language course on weekends since March.
The user's language instructor is Ms. Johnson.
The user plans to join the university's photography club and volunteer at the local animal shelter.
The user has been following the university's social media accounts to stay updated about upcoming events and activities.
UC Berkeley requires international students to have health insurance that covers at least $100,000 in medical expenses, $25,000 for repatriation, and $50,000 for medical evacuation.
UC Berkeley offers a Student Health Insurance Plan (SHIP) that meets the university's minimum health insurance requirements.
The user is eligible to enroll in UC Berkeley's Student Health Insurance Plan (SHIP) as an exchange student.
UC Berkeley has over 1,200 student organizations covering various interests, hobbies, and causes.
